name: HiPlot (High-Dimensional Interactive Visualization)

performance.notes: WebGL parallel coordinates; handles thousands of experiments efficiently

agent_readiness.notes: Parallel coordinates visualization for ML experiment tracking. import hiplot as hip. exp = hip.Experiment.from_dataframe(df). exp.display() (Jupyter). exp.to_html('results.html'). hip.Experiment.from_iterable([{'lr': 0.001, 'loss': 0.5, 'acc': 0.95}, ...]). Interactive: brush and link across dimensions. Color by column. Reorder axes by drag. Filter to subsets. exp.display_data(hip.Displays.HIPLOT). XAxis, YAxis config. hiplot.server for standalone server. Integrates with Streamlit. Parallel coordinates: each axis is a hyperparameter or metric.

gotchas: Limited outside ML hyperparameter context — very specialized tool
Requires Jupyter or standalone server for interactivity
Relatively niche — Optuna's visualization or Weights & Biases may be preferred in production
Less active development since Facebook Research released it
best_when: Visualizing ML hyperparameter search results with parallel coordinates
Comparing thousands of experiment runs interactively
Understanding correlations between hyperparameters and metrics
avoid_when: General data visualization (use plotly or altair)
Production ML tracking (use Weights & Biases, MLflow)
Non-Jupyter environments without standalone server setup
summary: HiPlot visualizes high-dimensional ML experiment data via parallel coordinates. hip.Experiment.from_dataframe(df).display(). Interactive brush and filter. HTML export. Standalone server. MIT by Meta. Best for exploring hyperparameter search results in Jupyter. Very specialized for ML experiment analysis. No type annotations. Less active than Weights & Biases or MLflow for production tracking.
